rankings.title: If I Don't Push My Kid, Will They Reach Their Potential? | Dr. Becky & Myleik Teele
summary_bullets: Parental push vs. child’s effort relationship
Separate your own baggage from child’s needs
Focus on effort process, not grades
Ask “What do you think is going on?”
Reframe: potential = effort + understanding
rankings.display_title: If I Don't Push My Kid, Will They Reach Their Potential? | Dr. Becky & Myleik Teele

rankings.title: What Every New Parent Needs to Know Before Having a Baby | Dr. Becky
summary_bullets: 66% of births don’t go as planned
8–12 feedings per 24 hours is normal
Cluster feeding can last 2 hours
1 in 5 mothers develop PPD/PPA
Zero guaranteed postpartum home visits in US
rankings.display_title: What Every New Parent Needs to Know Before Having a Baby | Dr. Becky

rankings.title: Postpartum OCD, Intrusive Thoughts: What’s Happening to Me? | Dr. Becky & Sabrina Rudin
summary_bullets: Postpartum OCD involves intrusive thoughts, not actions.
Husband normalized violent thoughts as human.
CBT helped separate thoughts from identity.
Fear of thoughts drives OCD, not the thoughts.
Sabrina learned to choose which thoughts to believe.
rankings.display_title: Postpartum OCD, Intrusive Thoughts: What’s Happening to Me? | Dr. Becky & Sabrina Rudin

rankings.title: "My Child Won't Talk About Their Feelings - What Can I Do?" | Dr. Sheryl
summary_bullets: Child avoids discussing feelings.
Mom uses guessing game to uncover issue.
Puberty complicates emotional expression.
Use high-interest topics to engage child.
“Mixed feelings” concept helps.
rankings.display_title: My Child Won't Talk About Their Feelings - What Can I Do? | Dr. Sheryl
